expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: REPORT_STATE and resume semantics remain stable
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the run cites Phase 0 contract coverage that includes “classify REPORT_STATE” and explicit “apply choice then route” semantics: options (full_regenerate/smart_refresh/reuse) followed by required `scripts/apply_user_choice.sh <mode> <feature-id> <run-dir>` and routing rules. It also quotes reference.md definitions for REPORT_STATE values (FINAL_EXISTS/DRAFT_EXISTS/CONTEXT_ONLY/FRESH) and selected_mode effects (full_regenerate clears all and returns to Phase 0; smart_refresh clears drafts and returns to Phase 2; reuse/resume continues), which directly encodes stable resume/idempotent behavior.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase phase0
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly frames the verdict as “PASS (phase0 contract coverage)”, states “This benchmark case checks that Phase 0 ... covers ... REPORT_STATE classification ... resume semantics”, and grounds evidence in SKILL.md “Phase Contract → Phase 0” plus reference.md runtime state tables. execution_notes.md also limits scope to phase contract evidence and notes no scripts were executed, consistent with a phase0 contract alignment rather than later-phase execution.
